steps.title: Identify the comparison structure
steps.content: The poem opens with a simile: “Like this alabaster box... is my heart.” This directly establishes two subjects being compared—one physical (the alabaster box) and one abstract (the heart). This is the decisive signal about what the underlined portions do.

steps.title: Match the subjects to the answer
steps.content:
The poem sustains the comparison through parallel descriptors applied to both subjects: both are frail, delicate, carved with thoughts and dreams.

This confirms A's claim that the comparison “extends throughout the text.” B wrongly frames this as a contrast between attitudes; C and D introduce non-existent narratives about a decision and a change.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
